Title: God's Song (That's Why I Love Mankind) - 2002 Remaster
Ana's Song (Open Fire) - Acoustic
Comment: This was a song about the lead singers battle with an eating disorder and I always thought was beautifully written. It really gets across the desperation he felt and how even though he knew it was killing him he still felt he needed it. The "sandpaper years corode the film" reference to the effect vomiting so often had on his throat I always found to be such a poetic way to phrase something frankly grotesque.

Title: Chop Suey!
Comment:
Clearly there’s some sort of biblical references in here. The ending ‘why have you forsaken me’ is pretty powerful wherever you take the meaning from though. 
I assume it’s talking about Jesus on the cross but relating it to someone else’s suicide as well. 
Makeup talking about how they present themselves to their peers vs how they feel inside under the makeup.

Title: Sunday Mornin' Comin' Down
Comment:
“All those words in the dictionary but no one else before Kris knew what damn order they came in.” - Larry Gatlin

An example all Nashville writers should mimic. You’re placed in the South even though he doesn’t say it directly. Allusions to church/religion while still authentic and relatable. Common country music loneliness theme and alcohol references without feeling contrived. All from a hippie Rhodes scholar from California.

Title: Dance With Somebody
Comment:
The arena goes dark. The music starts and echoes throughout. 

The announcers are distraught. What’s going on!? Who turned out the lights!

Starting @1:02
🎵with somebody who loves me🎵

Neon lights. Pastel leotard. Big 80s sunglasses. Curly blonde mullet. Dark tan. Reebok high tops. 

Neon Nasty appears out of the darkness. Gives a couple good pelvic thrusts, and rubs his face into the bosom of the closest overweight lady in the crowd.

Title: The Ballad Of Peter Pumpkinhead (feat. Ellen Reid)
Album: Playlist: The Very Best Of Crash Test Dummies
Comment: Magpie enters to a very quirky and distinctive intro. Magpie is an oddball that aligns with no one, and although fans love him they all agree something ain’t right. His appearances are random but highly consequential as he tends to disrupt others’ matches. Before he enters, announcers often say things like “this one is all but over. The only thing that’d save him now is if that wacko Magpie showed up and I don’t see tha-***guitar riff, drums, ‘Let’s Begin’***wah?! No! It can’t be!”

Title: Ali Ali
Comment:
A champion of the Curry Clash Smackdown, The Maharajah of Mayhem has come all the way from India to make butter chicken out of the jabronis in America. 

Ali Ali Ali begins…

He enters listening from one side of the stadium and hears the retort of the song from the other side. 

He runs down the aisle and enters the ring. 

Suddenly from above - a group of Indian dancers lowers and they have a traditional Indian dance off. At the end everyone yells Talley Ho in unison. 

The crowd goes wild.

Title: Brass Monkey
Comment: Johnny Swagg is an arrogant dick. He clearly wants to be the next Scott Hall but he comes across as more of a douche and less of a bad boy. To the great chagrin of fans everywhere he finds himself on the winning side often and has made his way to the elite ranks, but hardly on his own merits and instead by riding the coattails of the truly great bad boys.

Title: The Birdwatcher
Comment: As Vulfman enters the stadium the crowds reaction is mixed, never knowing what to expect from this enigmatic wrestler who seems equally content flying south nosediving in for the kill. Is this song fear inducing, of course not, but this little earworm will be stuck in your head like the one he caught at 5 am, meticulously honing his craft. As he flutters through the ring taking strategic flights, issuing melodic taunts, the fans will demand the sports great signature move, the Vulf Peck.

Title: Cocaine Jesus
Comment: Jesus, hellfire and brimstone, and as it turns out enough cocaine to kill Keith Richards. Disgraced 700 club televangelist "Brimstone" Bob Hagerty famous mission trip to Juarez Mexico ended with the St. Lucia Children's Home missing $250k of donations and Brimstone thundering down the 110 in a $150k Mercedes with a $1,500 an hour escort and a kilo of cocaine. But, heaven's loss is the WWE's gain as live tonight he faces Ric Flare in a walkers and canes showdown!

Title: Hey You
Comment: The once beloved sweetheart of the smash hit children's show "Super Duper Nova Time" Leigh Titsworth has long shed the good girl image that made her one of America's most loved child stars and now in a desperate attempt to feed her spiraling drug problem she is joining the WWE! Join us tonight as she battles former Polynesian adult star Kinda Wanna Layu in an impractically small outfit in a battle that will likely involve either water, oil, whip cream, or all of the above!

Title: Black Skinhead
Comment: Ye enters to universal boos. You hate him, his opponents hate him, he seems to hate himself. Nothing matters because Ye is a winner and if he hasn't won, then the match isn't over. Cheating, quitting, hiding, getting knocked out, none of this matters for the sports least redeemable villain. Oh this song is offensive, Ye knows, he wants it that way. But from the first beat the the last refrain this song slaps and YOU KNOW IT!  He'll cross lines you didn't know existed in the ring and yourself.
